question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogsById()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/:contractId/results/logs?timestamp=gte:A&timestamp=lte:A (same value for gte and lte) to trigger alterTimestampRange() which collapses them into a single eq filter, then cause checkTimestampsForTopics() to pass, and then trigger an exact-timestamp log query that may still be expensive if many logs exist at that timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResultsByTransactionIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/results/<valid_transaction_id> (non-hash path) to trigger TransactionService.getTransactionDetailsFromTransactionId() followed by getInvolvedContractsByTimestampAndContractId() and then 5 parallel getDetailedContractResults() queries, totaling 7 DB queries per single HTTP request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResultsByTransactionIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/results/<valid_eth_hash> where the matched ethereum transaction has a valid callDataId, causing FileDataService.getLatestFileDataContents() to fetch large file data, adding memory pressure on top of the 6 existing DB queries per request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractById()] Can an unprivileged external user send many concurrent GET /contracts/<evm_address> requests where the EVM address does not exist, causing computeContractIdFromString() to issue a DB lookup via getContractIdByEvmAddress() for each request, exhausting DB connections even though all requests result in NotFoundError?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResultsById()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/<evm_address>/results to trigger computeContractIdFromString() which calls getContractIdByEvmAddress() (DB query), then extractContractResultsByIdQuery() which may call optimizeTimestampFilters() and bindTimestampRange() (another DB query), then getContractResultsByIdAndFilters() (third DB query), totaling 3 DB queries per request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractStateById()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/<evm_address>/state to trigger computeContractIdFromString() (DB query for EVM address), then getContractStateByIdAndFilters() (second DB query), chaining two DB queries per request and enabling DoS through concurrent requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogsById()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/<evm_address>/results/logs to trigger computeContractIdFromString() (DB query), then extractContractLogsMultiUnionQuery() which may call optimizeTimestampFilters() and bindTimestampRange() (second DB query), then getContractLogs() (third DB query), totaling 3 DB queries per request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResults()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/results?from=<evm_address> where the from value is an EVM address, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to be called (a DB lookup) inside extractContractResultsByIdQuery() before the main query, adding an extra DB query per request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResultsById()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/:contractId/results?from=<evm_addr1>&from=<evm_addr2>&...&from=<evm_addrN> with many EVM address from values, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to be called N times sequentially inside the filter loop, resulting in N extra DB queries before the main query executes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractSqlFromContractFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts?contract_id=<evm_addr1>&contract_id=<evm_addr2>&...&contract_id=<evm_addrN> with many EVM address contract_id values, causing ContractService.computeContractIdFromString() to be called N times inside the filter loop, each triggering a DB lookup, resulting in N sequential DB queries before the main contracts query?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractActions()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/results/<valid_transaction_id>/actions?index=gte:0&index=lte:0&index=gte:1&index=lte:1&...&index=gte:N&index=lte:N with many index range filters, causing getContractActions() in contractService to build a WHERE clause with many AND conditions, resulting in an expensive DB query?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogsById()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/:contractId/results/logs?index=gte:0&index=lte:999999&timestamp=gte:A&timestamp=lte:B to trigger a multi-union query with both timestamp and index bounds, causing getContractLogsLowerFilters(), getInnerFilters(), and getUpperFilters() to each generate separate SQL sub-queries that are UNIONed together, resulting in a very expensive DB operation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/results/logs?index=gte:0&index=lte:999999&timestamp=gte:A&timestamp=lte:B to trigger the same multi-union query pattern as getContractLogsById() but without a contractId constraint, causing a full-table multi-union scan across all contract logs?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResultsByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate valid (contractId, consensusTimestamp) pairs from public blockchain data and then send many concurrent GET /contracts/:contractId/results/:consensusTimestamp requests, each triggering 6+ DB queries via getDetailedContractResults(), causing sustained DB connection pool exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResults()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/results?block_hash=<valid_hash> to trigger RecordFileService.getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromHash() (DB query), then if found, add timestamp filters and call optimizeTimestampFilters() (potentially another DB query via bindTimestampRange()), then getContractResultsByIdAndFilters(), then two parallel queries for ethereum transactions and record files, totaling 5+ DB queries per request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResults()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/results?block_number=<N> to trigger RecordFileService.getRecordFileBlockDetailsFromIndex() (DB query), then timestamp-bounded queries, then two parallel DB queries for ethereum transactions and record files, totaling 4+ DB queries per request, enabling DoS through concurrent requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractById()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/:contractId?timestamp=gte:A&timestamp=lte:B where A and B span a very wide range, causing getContractByIdOrAddressContractEntityQuery() to UNION query both entity and entity_history tables with a broad timestamp condition, resulting in a full history table scan that exhausts DB I/O?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogsById()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/:contractId/results/logs?transaction_hash=<valid_hash>&timestamp=gte:A&timestamp=lte:B to trigger getTransactionHash() (DB query) followed by a timestamp-bounded log query, chaining two DB queries per request and enabling DoS through concurrent requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/results/logs?transaction_hash=<valid_hash> to trigger getTransactionHash() (DB query) and then a timestamp-exact log query, and by sending many concurrent requests with different valid hashes, exhaust the DB connection pool causing non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractStateById()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/:contractId/state?timestamp=lte:<T>&slot=<v1>&slot=<v2>&...&slot=<vN> with many slot values and a timestamp filter, causing extractContractStateByIdQuery() to call formatSlot() N times with timestamp-mode conversion and build a large IN clause against the contract_state_change table, resulting in an expensive historical state query?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getContractResultsById()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /contracts/:contractId/results?timestamp=gte:A&timestamp=lte:B&timestamp=ne:C1&timestamp=ne:C2&...&timestamp=ne:CN with many ne timestamp exclusions, causing extractContractResultsByIdQuery() to pass all ne values through to the DB query as multiple AND conditions, resulting in an expensive filtered scan?
